profile.name: Daniel
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; self-employed; tends to lead on investing and big financial decisions
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (comfortable with account types, interest rates, and trade-offs; tends to track balances and ask for specifics)
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but analytical—willing to take market risk for growth if the plan is structured; gets uneasy when cash flow feels tight because of large fixed payments
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-oriented; will quote numbers, ask 'what does that mean in dollars per month?', and correct small inaccuracies. Uses occasional hedges like 'roughly' or 'if I’m remembering right,' but generally wants precision.
emotional_tendencies: stress spikes around large recurring bills
relief when there is a clear checklist/plan
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-verify; prefers to compare options side-by-side and decide after seeing projections and assumptions
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting—open to guidance but expects transparency on fees, risks, and rationale
internal_biases: anchoring to interest rates when evaluating debt payoff vs investing
overconfidence in ability to 'figure it out' due to being self-directed
hidden_concerns: worries about maintaining lifestyle and savings momentum with high monthly obligations
concerned about being adequately protected for the kids if something happens

profile.name: Linda
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; manages day-to-day family logistics and spending priorities; participates actively but often defers on investment mechanics
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands budgeting and goals; less fluent in investment/insurance terminology and may mix up premium vs benefit or balance vs payment)
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-leaning conservative in practice—supports growth goals but prefers stability and a bigger cash cushion; reacts more to short-term volatility and 'what if' scenarios
profile.communication_style: Warm, conversational, and big-picture; speaks in round numbers ('about', 'around'), asks clarifying questions, and occasionally interrupts to confirm practical impacts. Uses fillers like 'I mean' and 'kind of' and may say 'I’m not totally sure' on account details.
emotional_tendencies: guilt when spending feels high even if it’s planned
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to feel heard and to understand how choices affect the household month-to-month before agreeing
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting if the advisor is patient and explains without jargon; trust increases with clear summaries and follow-ups
internal_biases: loss aversion (prefers avoiding regret over maximizing returns)
mental accounting (separates 'house money' vs 'retirement money' emotionally)
hidden_concerns: worries about the kids’ needs and timing of expenses as they get older
concerned that insurance costs might be high but unsure how to evaluate value
